title: Inhibition of FGFR Signaling Partially Rescues Osteoarthritis in Mice Overexpressing High Molecular Weight FGF2 Isoforms
research_question: Does in vivo treatment with the FGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor NVP-BGJ398 modulate the development of osteoarthritis (OA) phenotype in HMWTg mice overexpressing high molecular weight FGF2 isoforms?
methods_summary: Transgenic mouse model (HMWTg mice overexpressing HMWFGF2 isoforms in osteoblast lineage)
In vivo treatment with FGFR inhibitor NVP-BGJ398 (2 mg/kg, daily subcutaneous injection for 13 weeks starting at 21 days of age)
Histological assessment (Safranin-O staining, alkaline phosphatase staining, OARSI scoring)
Real-time quantitative RT-PCR (qRT-PCR) for gene expression
Immunohistochemistry for protein expression and localization
Serum FGF23 and estradiol measurement by ELISA
dataset: HMWTg transgenic mice and VectorTg control mice (FVBN strain), both male and female, treated with vehicle or BGJ (n=7-9 males/group, n=7-8 females/group)
key_findings: BGJ treatment partially rescued radiographic and microCT abnormalities (irregular subchondral bone, decreased BV/TV, Tb.Th, Tb.N) in HMWTg mice, especially in males.
BGJ restored articular cartilage thickness and proteoglycan content, and reduced OARSI scores in HMWTg mice.
BGJ decreased OA degradative enzymes (MMP13, ADAMTS5) and hypertrophic markers (Col10, SOX9) in HMWTg joints.
BGJ reduced increased pFGFR1, FGF23, and Wnt signaling components (WNT7b, pLRP5, active β-catenin, AXIN2, pGSK3β) while restoring Wnt inhibitors (DKK1, SOST) in HMWTg cartilage.
BGJ had negative effects on bone parameters in female VectorTg mice but did not alter serum estradiol levels.
extracted_insights: Overexpression of HMWFGF2 isoforms in preosteoblasts leads to osteoarthropathy partially mediated by increased FGFR1 signaling and aberrant Wnt pathway activation. The FGFR inhibitor NVP-BGJ398 partially rescues the OA phenotype by reducing activated Wnt signaling, suggesting FGFR blockade as a potential therapeutic strategy for OA associated with XLH and related conditions.
